description_long: This is a two-handled ceramic vase with a striking anthropomorphic design, featuring a sculpted human face integrated into the lower portion of the vessel. The upper body of the vase is flared and painted in a dark, almost black slip, with areas of wear revealing the underlying clay. The handles are robust and symmetrically placed, arching from the rim to the shoulder of the vase. Decorative elements include painted dots and lines, as well as a second stylized face motif on the opposite side. The surface shows signs of age, with chips and abrasions visible throughout. The overall form and ornamentation suggest a blend of functional and artistic intent, making it a distinctive decorative object.
description: A two-handled ceramic vase with sculpted and painted human faces, dark slip finish, and visible wear. The vessel combines artistic and functional elements with detailed ornamentation.
description_view_0: Front view shows the main sculpted human face, detailed eyes, and decorative painted dots along the rim.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the thickness of the handle and the profile of the sculpted face, with visible wear on the dark slip.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals a second, more stylized painted face motif and the symmetrical placement of the handles.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile shows the other handle and the side of the sculpted face, with additional painted details visible.
